original_abstract: In the era of digital transformation, blockchain technology has emerged as a disruptive solution to critical challenges in healthcare, including data interoperability, patient privacy, and resource management. This systematic review explores the current landscape of blockchain applications in the health sector, focusing on documented success stories and implementation stages. Following PRISMA guidelines, 10 high-quality studies were selected from a pool of 723 articles indexed in databases such as Scopus, PubMed, and ScienceDirect. The analyzed research highlights a global interest in the technology—particularly in the United Arab Emirates, China, and India—addressing diverse areas such as electronic health record security, pharmaceutical supply chain integrity, medical waste tracking, and blood donation management. Results indicate that the field is balanced between theoretical modeling and practical implementation, with experimental phases demonstrating significant improvements in data encryption via smart contracts and decentralized storage. The study concludes that while blockchain offers robust benefits for transparency and fraud prevention in healthcare, its widespread adoption remains subject to ongoing developments in scalability and regulatory governance.
humanized_abstract: Objective Blockchain is a new technological revolution, which provides an innovative solution to the many health challenges such as interoperability, privacy, management of data and resources, etc. This review aimed at identifying the applications of Blockchain in health sector with special focus on the use cases, where the technology is successfully implemented and tested in a trial environment. We adhered to PRISMA guidelines and conducted this systematic review by searching academic publications, which have implemented real-world trials on Blockchain technology in the health sector. Our search was on top-rated scientific sources like Scopus, PubMed, ScienceDirect, etc., and after filtering 723 scientific studies, our findings reduced to 10 high-quality studies. The reviews of these studies reveal international interest in the development and applications of Blockchain in health sector across the globe and specifically in countries like United Arab Emirates, China and India. It further reveals the applications of Blockchain technology in various aspects of the health sector such as securing Electronic Health Records (EHRs), integrity in pharmaceutical supply chains, managing medical waste, as well as in the donation of blood. The studies relating to the experimental trials had designed all the aspects of blockchain technology in the health sector and the trials gave promising results. The data after decryption was effectively stored through the smart contracts and the data was highly secure due to the decentralized network given by the Blockchain technology. Conclusion Although blockchain technology has many benefits for healthcare sector such as providing information of health sector transparently, preventing fraud and many more, its adoption for the healthcare sector is on a trial basis as yet due to limitations and need of improvements in the performance of Blockchain technology, in the aspects of future scalability of the network, and the formulation of governing policies.
